message:
feat(runtime_compat): tick 205 output_with_cx watcher sleep uses sleep_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Command::output_with_cx spawns a watcher task that polls the
caller's cx for cancellation and flips an AtomicBool that the
blocking child-exec observes. The watcher code did the right
thing on cancel-check (`watcher_cx.is_cancel_requested()`) but the
inter-poll sleep used ambient `super::sleep(PROCESS_POLL_INTERVAL)`.

`super::sleep` falls back to `Cx::current()` thread-local for the
budget timer. In LabRuntime virtual-time tests, the watcher's
cancel-check and the sleep-timer were therefore bound to different
cx values — asymmetric ownership. The watcher would still notice
cancel eventually, but the timer wasn't part of the caller's
explicit cx budget.

Tick 205 routes the sleep through `super::sleep_with_cx(&watcher_cx,
PROCESS_POLL_INTERVAL)`. Now both the cancel check and the timer
observe the same cx clone the caller threaded in.

Verification: 194 / 194 runtime_compat:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target), including
process_command_output_with_cx_cancellation_surfaces_as_interrupted
— the exact test that exercises the cx-cancel bridge watcher.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
